title: Slow query with exact uri match
description:
Dear virtuoso team,
I am running virtuoso "version 7.2.4.2.3217-pthreads as of Apr 26 2016" (virtuoso-opensource git commit id: 0468195).
On our large dataset (uniprot 2017_07, >27 billions triples), I observe the following timing for some test queries:
##############
[someuser@someserver ~]$ date
Thu May  4 17:02:25 CEST 2017
[someuser@someserver ~]$ ./bin/isql xxxxx
[...]
SQL> sparql
#test THL for UPS-245 2017-05-04
#DEFINE sql:select-option "order"
PREFIX up:http://purl.uniprot.org/core/
PREFIX rdfs:http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#
SELECT     ?protein ?db ?detail
FROM http://sparql.uniprot.org/uniprot
WHERE
{ ?protein  up:reviewed           true ;
a                     up:Protein ;
rdfs:seeAlso          ?db .
?db       up:database           http://purl.uniprot.org/database/MGI
#?db       up:database           http://purl.uniprot.org/database/EcoGene
OPTIONAL
{ ?db  rdfs:comment  ?detail }
}
LIMIT   1;
protein                                                                           db                                                                                detail

http://purl.uniprot.org/uniprot/Q8VBW1 http://purl.uniprot.org/mgi/2147834                                               Slc6a8
1 Rows. -- 1068 msec.
SQL>
SQL>
SQL>
SQL> sparql
#test THL for UPS-245 2017-05-04
DEFINE sql:select-option "order"
PREFIX up:http://purl.uniprot.org/core/
PREFIX rdfs:http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#
SELECT     ?protein ?db ?detail
FROM http://sparql.uniprot.org/uniprot
WHERE
{ ?protein  up:reviewed           true ;
a                     up:Protein ;
rdfs:seeAlso          ?db .
?db       up:database           http://purl.uniprot.org/database/MGI
#?db       up:database           http://purl.uniprot.org/database/EcoGene
OPTIONAL
{ ?db  rdfs:comment  ?detail }
}
LIMIT   1;
protein                                                                           db                                                                                detail

http://purl.uniprot.org/uniprot/Q80TV8 http://purl.uniprot.org/mgi/1923957                                               Clasp1
1 Rows. -- 24 msec.
SQL>
SQL>
SQL>
SQL> sparql
#650 test THL for UPS-245 2017-05-04
#DEFINE sql:select-option "order"
PREFIX up:http://purl.uniprot.org/core/
PREFIX rdfs:http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#
SELECT     ?protein ?db ?detail
FROM http://sparql.uniprot.org/uniprot
WHERE
{ ?protein  up:reviewed           true ;
a                     up:Protein ;
rdfs:seeAlso          ?db .
#?db       up:database           http://purl.uniprot.org/database/MGI
?db       up:database           http://purl.uniprot.org/database/EcoGene
OPTIONAL
{ ?db  rdfs:comment  ?detail }
}
LIMIT   1;
protein                                                                           db                                                                                detail

http://purl.uniprot.org/uniprot/P00350 http://purl.uniprot.org/ecogene/EG10411                                           gnd
1 Rows. -- 147069 msec.
SQL>
SQL>
SQL>
SQL> sparql
#test THL for UPS-245 2017-05-04
DEFINE sql:select-option "order"
PREFIX up:http://purl.uniprot.org/core/
PREFIX rdfs:http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#
SELECT     ?protein ?db ?detail
FROM http://sparql.uniprot.org/uniprot
WHERE
{ ?protein  up:reviewed           true ;
a                     up:Protein ;
rdfs:seeAlso          ?db .
#?db       up:database           http://purl.uniprot.org/database/MGI
?db       up:database           http://purl.uniprot.org/database/EcoGene
OPTIONAL
{ ?db  rdfs:comment  ?detail }
}
LIMIT   1;
protein                                                                           db                                                                                detail

http://purl.uniprot.org/uniprot/P75682 http://purl.uniprot.org/ecogene/EG13344                                           yagE
1 Rows. -- 50 msec.
SQL>
##############
The dataset is uniprot version 2017_04.
The results are correct, but I see two distinct performance issues:
Issue1: As you can see, changing the uri match between the 1st and the 3rd query (only difference is "...EcoGene>" instead of "...MGI>" makes the query significantly longer (>2 minutes vs ~1 sec). Interestingly, there are less uri matching "...EcoGene>" than uri matching "...MGI>" in the dataset (data not shown).
Issue2: Adding DEFINE sql:select-option "order" makes both cases faster (less than 100ms with this option). This suggests that the query optimizer is not selecting the best join order.
Remark: I also tested these queries in cold/warm situations (after complete virtuoso-t restart / and on a server running for days after running thousands of queries ) and the timing remain almost the same. Removing the OPTIONAL part of the query doesn't change the above observations significantly.
The Issue1 is the most annoying. Can you please have a look at what is happening here?
(internal ref: UPS-245)
key_bug_features: Changing URI match significantly affects query performance
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE proteins (id INT PRIMARY KEY, uri VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, reviewed BOOLEAN, detail TEXT);
INSERT INTO proteins (id, uri, reviewed, detail) VALUES (1, 'http://purl.uniprot.org/uniprot/Q8VBW1', TRUE, 'Slc6a8');
INSERT INTO proteins (id, uri, reviewed, detail) VALUES (2, 'http://purl.uniprot.org/uniprot/P00350', TRUE, NULL);
SELECT * FROM proteins WHERE reviewed = TRUE AND uri LIKE 'http://purl.uniprot.org/uniprot/%' AND detail IS NOT NULL;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM proteins WHERE uri IN (SELECT uri FROM proteins WHERE reviewed = TRUE) AND reviewed = TRUE;
